_comment: Agent-facing compensation workflow. Each action is a Directus MANUAL flow triggered via POST /flows/trigger/{id} with body { collection: 'compensation_requests', keys: [<requestId>], ...inputs }. Most actions are one-click (inputs: []). A few prod flows require the operator to fill fields in Directus' manual-trigger dialog — those SAME fields are declared in `inputs` and collected by the portal (apps/agent-portal/src/features/compensation/actions.ts), then sent with the trigger, so nothing is typed in Directus. Flow IDs are identical in production and the local clone (preserved on purpose) so the portal is environment-agnostic — it targets VITE_DIRECTUS_URL. Local stand-in flows (standin-flows.mjs) mirror the prod pipelines stage-for-stage (item-read -> exec -> item-update, item-create) to produce the exact same status/field transitions, but make NO external calls. `key` values map to standin-flows.mjs's PIPELINES table; do not rename without updating it. Input `type` mirrors the Directus field type; `kind` (in the portal's actions.ts) mirrors how it renders. Prod pipelines are snapshotted read-only in schema/prod-flow-*.json.
_statuses_comment: EXACT prod status enum (compensation_requests.status, default Pending). Only Acknowledge/Approve/Reject change status; Calculate/Generate/Assign set fields+flags without moving status; Refund (Close task) has no prod operations and no effect.

actions.label: Acknowledge
actions.effect: status -> In Progress; stamps status_date, inprogress_at, inprogress_by, inprogress_sla_violation, inprogress_sla_minutes, updated_at, date_updated

actions.label: Calculate compensation
actions.effect: NO status change; sets request_frequency, suggested_compensation_value, final_compensation_value, calculate_compensation=true (prod runs a frequency + rules engine; local derives value from claimed amount)

actions.label: Generate coupon
actions.effect: NO status change; creates a Com_Coupons row from the inputs (Name+Code) and links it via `coupons` (portal reads coupons.Code). PROD additionally POSTs to the Yiji AddCoupon HTTP API — NOT replicated locally — and writes a phantom `generate_coupon` flag (a non-readable prod column) which is omitted locally.

actions.label: Assign coupon
actions.effect: NO status change; re-links the already-linked coupon (idempotent). Prod also writes the phantom `generate_coupon` flag (omitted locally), so there is no distinct observable change.

actions.label: Accept
actions.effect: status -> Approved; stamps status_date, approved_at, solved_sla_violation, solved_sla_minutes. Prod also writes phantom `approved_by` (a non-readable prod column), omitted locally.

actions.label: Reject
actions.effect: status -> Rejected; stamps status_date, declined_at, declined_by; stores reason in decline_reason

actions.label: Close task
actions.effect: NO effect — prod's 'CR->Refund amount' flow has ZERO operations. The optional reason field exists on the trigger but nothing is written.
